subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Main game range** — The core game flow is presented for **3-7 players**.
- **2-player support** — Box includes **2 "2 player" cards** and an **Expert variant for 2 players**.
- **Age deck filtering** — Use only cards marked up to current player count (e.g., 6-player game uses 3+, 4+, 5+, 6+; remove 7+).
- **Age III guild scaling** — Keep guild count equal to **players + 2**.
- **Guild count by players** — Explicit rulebook mapping:
  - 3 Players -> 5 Guilds
  - 4 Players -> 6 Guilds
  - 5 Players -> 7 Guilds
  - 6 Players -> 8 Guilds
  - 7 Players -> 9 Guilds

#### Box Contents
- **Wonder boards** — 7.
- **Wonder cards** — 7.
- **Age I cards** — 49.
- **Age II cards** — 49.
- **Age III cards** — 50.
- **Conflict tokens** — 46.
- **Value-3 coins** — 24.
- **Value-1 coins** — 46.
- **Score booklet** — 1.
- **Rulebook** — 1.
- **2-player cards** — 2.

#### Card Families (Color Reference)
- **Brown** — Raw materials.
- **Gray** — Manufactured goods.
- **Blue** — Civilian VP cards.
- **Green** — Science cards.
- **Yellow** — Commerce cards.
- **Red** — Military cards.
- **Purple** — Guild cards (Age III only).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Layout Sequence
1. **Place reserves** — Put coin bank and conflict token reserve in reach of all players.
2. **Prepare Age I deck** — Remove cards above player count, then shuffle.
3. **Prepare Age II deck** — Remove cards above player count, then shuffle.
4. **Prepare Age III deck** — Remove cards above player count, keep correct guild count (players + 2), then shuffle guilds into Age III.
5. **Assign wonders** — Shuffle wonder cards and deal one per player, or choose boards by agreement.
6. **Place player cities** — Each player places wonder board in front of them.

#### Age III Guild Setup by Player Count
--- 3 Players ---
- **Guilds kept** — 5.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Guilds kept** — 6.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Guilds kept** — 7.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Guilds kept** — 8.

--- 7 Players ---
- **Guilds kept** — 9.

#### Card-Filter Rule by Player Count
- **Filter key** — Use cards marked with your player count or lower (3+ up to current count).
- **Example (from rulebook)** — In 6 players, use 3+, 4+, 5+, 6+ and remove 7+.

#### 2-Player Variant Note
--- 2 Players ---
- **Variant exists** — Rulebook contains an Expert 2-player variant.
- **Core deck base** — Variant uses 3-player card configuration (3+ cards only).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Player Setup
- **Starting coins** — Each player starts with 3 value-1 coins.
- **Starting resource** — Each wonder board provides one printed starting resource.
- **Board side guidance** — Side A is recommended for first games.
- **Bank handling** — Remaining coins form bank; players may make change between 1 and 3 values.

#### Start-of-Age Deal
- **Hand size** — At the start of each age, each player receives 7 cards from that age deck.
- **Visibility** — Hands are private until simultaneous reveal.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 3-7 Players ---
- **Core setup** — Use standard setup above.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Variant caution** — Rulebook recommends learning with 3-7 players first.
- **Variant setup** — Uses additional variant rules and components (including 2-player cards and Free City handling).

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game Start Conditions
- **Age order** — Play Age I, then Age II, then Age III.
- **Turns per age** — 6 turns per age.
- **Simultaneous play** — All players choose and resolve actions simultaneously.

#### Hand Passing Direction
- **Age I** — Pass left (clockwise).
- **Age II** — Pass right (counter-clockwise).
- **Age III** — Pass left (clockwise).

#### Sixth-Turn Special Rule
- **Two-card hand** — On turn 6, each player receives 2 cards.
- **Play one card** — Choose one to resolve normally.
- **Discard one card** — Unchosen card is discarded face down.
- **No coin gain** — You do not gain 3 coins for that unchosen card.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 3-7 Players ---
- **Core start flow** — Use standard simultaneous age flow.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Core timing** — Variant still follows age structure, with special variant adjustments.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence (Standard Ages)
1. **Choose card** — Every player selects one card from hand and places it face down.
2. **Resolve action** — All players reveal and resolve one action simultaneously.
3. **Pass hand** — Pass remaining hand according to current age direction.
4. **Repeat** — Continue until each player has played 6 cards in that age.
5. **Resolve military** — At age end, compare military with both neighbors.
6. **Next age / end** — Start next age, or after Age III move to final scoring.

#### Action Choices (each turn)
- **Build structure** — Play card into city by paying its cost or chain condition.
- **Build wonder stage** — Tuck a card under wonder and pay wonder-stage cost.
- **Discard for coins** — Discard chosen card for 3 coins.

#### Sixth-Turn Exception
- **Input size** — 2 cards received.
- **Output** — Play 1, discard 1 face down with no 3-coin reward.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 3-7 Players ---
- **Core sequence** — Standard simultaneous sequence applies.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Variant sequence** — Uses 2-player expert adjustments while preserving age structure.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action A: Build a Structure
- **Cost payment** — Pay printed coin/resource cost unless free by chain.
- **Duplicate rule** — You cannot build two identical structures in your city.
- **Placement** — Brown/gray usually grouped for production visibility; other colors in city area.

#### Resource Rules
- **Persistent production** — Resources are not spent; production remains every turn.
- **Sources** — Wonder board, brown cards, gray cards, and some yellow cards.

#### Commerce Rules
- **Neighbors only** — Buy missing resources only from immediate left/right neighbors.
- **Buyable sources** — Neighbor wonder starting resource + brown + gray production.
- **Restricted sources** — Some yellow/wonder resources are owner-only and not purchasable.
- **Default price** — 2 coins per resource bought.
- **Discount effects** — Some yellow cards reduce buy cost from 2 to 1.
- **Cannot refuse sale** — Neighbors must sell available resources.
- **Turn timing** — Bought resources are valid only this turn.
- **Coin timing** — Coins earned this turn cannot be spent for same-turn purchases.

#### Action B: Build a Wonder Stage
- **Card use** — Use selected card as construction marker (face down under wonder).
- **Cost source** — Pay wonder-stage cost, not card cost.
- **Order rule** — Stages must be built left to right.

#### Action C: Discard for Coins
- **Reward** — Gain 3 coins from bank.
- **Sixth-turn limit** — Unchosen 6th-turn card gives no 3-coin reward.

#### Free Chain Construction
- **Condition** — Some Age II/III cards show a prerequisite from earlier age.
- **Effect** — If prerequisite exists in your city, build for free.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Simultaneous Drafting Engine
- **Core pressure** — What you choose affects what your neighbors receive next.
- **Denial value** — Taking a card can be correct even when it is not your top personal score card.

#### Military Conflict System
- **Timing** — Resolve at end of each age.
- **Comparison** — Compare shields with left neighbor and right neighbor separately.
- **Win token values** — +1 in Age I, +3 in Age II, +5 in Age III.
- **Loss token value** — -1 defeat token.

#### Color-Lane Specialization
- **Brown/Gray** — Production base.
- **Yellow** — Economy and commerce rule changes.
- **Blue** — Direct VP.
- **Green** — Science scoring engine.
- **Red** — Military leverage.
- **Purple** — End-game conditional scoring.

#### Guild Scaling by Player Count
--- 3 Players ---
- **Guilds in Age III** — 5.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Guilds in Age III** — 6.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Guilds in Age III** — 7.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Guilds in Age III** — 8.

--- 7 Players ---
- **Guilds in Age III** — 9.

#### 2-Player Expert Variant Presence
--- 2 Players ---
- **Variant status** — Expert variant exists with Free City and border-card adjustments.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Game end point** — End of Age III after military conflict resolution.

#### Final Scoring Categories
1. **Military tokens** — Add victory tokens and subtract defeat tokens.
2. **Coins** — Score 1 VP per 3 coins (round down).
3. **Blue cards** — Add printed civilian VP.
4. **Green cards** — Score science according to science rules.
5. **Yellow cards** — Add VP from commerce effects.
6. **Purple guilds** — Add guild-specific VP.
7. **Wonder stages** — Add wonder-stage VP/effects.

#### Winner & Tie Rule
- **Winner** — Highest total VP.
- **Tie-breaker** — Most coins wins tied VP.
- **If still tied** — Shared victory unless table uses a house rule.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 3-7 Players ---
- **Standard end flow** — Use the scoring categories above.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Variant end flow** — Apply 2-player expert variant adjustments plus normal category scoring.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Economy first** — Build brown/gray resource base early in Age I.
- **Coin buffer** — Keep enough coins to avoid dead turns on good drafts.
- **Neighbor read** — Track both neighbors’ production and military direction.
- **Chain prep** — Build at least one useful chain starter in Age I.
- **Wonder timing** — Use wonder stages when draft hand is weak for structure play.

#### Player-Count Focus
--- 3 Players ---
- **Military map** — Two-neighbor military race is highly swingy; avoid repeated -1 losses.
- **Guild density** — 5 guilds means narrower purple options; draft denial matters.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Military pressure** — Still two-neighbor comparisons; red tempo can punish passive openings.
- **Guild density** — 6 guilds broadens late-game options.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Draft noise** — Hand quality shifts faster; prioritize flexible economy.
- **Guild density** — 7 guilds increase conditional scoring opportunities.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Draft denial** — Stronger need to deny immediate neighbor spikes.
- **Guild density** — 8 guilds make purple reads more important.

--- 7 Players ---
- **Table speed** — Keep decisions concise to preserve simultaneous tempo.
- **Guild density** — 9 guilds maximize purple volatility.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Prerequisite** — Rulebook advises learning 3-7 player flow first.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1: Age I point greed
- **Problem** — Early blue scoring without production causes stalled Ages II-III.
- **Fix** — Secure resource base first.

#### Mistake 2: Commerce timing errors
- **Problem** — Spending coins earned this turn on same-turn purchases is illegal.
- **Fix** — Budget turn-start coins before selecting build.

#### Mistake 3: Treating bought resources as permanent
- **Problem** — Bought resources only apply this turn.
- **Fix** — Recompute buy needs every turn.

#### Mistake 4: Ignoring military bleed
- **Problem** — Repeated -1 tokens quietly erase endgame gains.
- **Fix** — Hit parity with neighbors when possible.

#### Mistake 5: Duplicate structure errors
- **Problem** — Same-name structure cannot be built twice.
- **Fix** — Verify title/illustration before committing card.

#### Mistake 6: Zero denial drafting
- **Problem** — Passing obvious chain/guild spikes to neighbors loses EV.
- **Fix** — Include neighbor-impact check before final pick.

#### Player-Count Notes
--- 3-7 Players ---
- **Common pitfall** — Forgetting to re-filter age decks by player count each setup.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Common pitfall** — Mixing standard and expert-variant procedures.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Economy depth vs immediate VP
- **Economy line** — Better long-run consistency and build freedom.
- **VP line** — Faster visible score but higher stall risk later.

#### Decision 2: Wonder stage vs structure card
- **Wonder line** — Good conversion when card is low-value in city.
- **Structure line** — Better when card improves engine or blocks opponent.

#### Decision 3: Military threshold vs military push
- **Threshold line** — Invest enough for ties/wins without overcommitting.
- **Push line** — Commit harder to capture larger age-end token swings.

#### Decision 4: Science commitment level
- **Commit line** — Build symbol plan early and support it with chains/resources.
- **Opportunistic line** — Take green only when it fits without forcing.

#### Decision 5: Coin liquidity vs card quality
- **Liquidity line** — Discard for 3 coins to unlock stronger next turns.
- **Quality line** — Keep board growth now if economy already stable.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 3 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Fewer guilds (5) make purple denial highly tactical.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Guild count (6) expands endgame pivots moderately.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Guild count (7) raises value of late color balancing.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Guild count (8) increases conditional-score competition.

--- 7 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Guild count (9) makes purple table-read critical.

--- 2 Players (Expert Variant) ---
- **Key trade-off** — Variant board flow (Free City / border-card dynamics) changes denial calculus.

metadata.notes: Written from provided 7 Wonders rulebook text excerpt as primary source.
